text:

Hi Marki,

You've trained 1M+ agents and judged NAR pitches - you know what actually works vs what sounds good in demos.

I'm a licensed agent (and Microsoft PM) building a voice-first platform for agents. 𝗣𝗵𝗮𝘀𝗲 1: Practice FSBO cold calls and buyer consults with AI that pushes back like real objections. A full Voice forward CRM that works while driving with all comms in one place.

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗶𝗴𝗴𝗲𝗿 𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻: Full productivity platform that removes the friction between agents and their actual job - selling.

15 𝗺𝗶𝗻𝘂𝘁𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲? I need honest feedback from someone who's seen what agents actually struggle with at scale.

And if you're free at NAR, would love to meet in person.

No deck. Just a lively conversatioin.

Thanks,
Bryan Plaza

No, but thanks for the lead! I’ve been of the mind to just connect with agents I know and build something that could be useful to them and this came up. I’m still working on market research since this is a new idea. 

Frankly, also thought about doing prop tech consulting for a bit to really identify some pain points and opportunities.

I have been deep on image models lately because of my corporate job. I also have a deeper understanding of generating documents like Word Excel and PowerPoint files. So my current investment is creating a listing kit tool for real estate agent agents in order to create all of the assets, and they may need for a listing in one go. The goal is to consolidate a bunch of other tools that are out there in the market into one platform. Think of combining things like AI generated video tours, AI staging and other AI marketing assets.

I would actually love to see what you’re working on! How about this? I am working on a product myself and I am consulting to get better understanding of pain points in the industry. I’m happy to check in to get some feedback and learn more about what you’re doing once I have a better footing.

Hey Leo!

I'm excited to be on the panel with you today - can't wait to have that conversation! I saw you made the jump from Tesla to full-time real estate operating. That's a bold move!

I'm curious about your take on AI for passive investing. Most of the AI tools I'm seeing are for active operators, but your investors want hands-off solutions. Are you finding ways to use AI to give passive investors better visibility without them having to actually do the work?

Coming from enterprise tech, I've seen how automation can completely change the investor experience. Would love to compare notes on what's working for democratizing access beyond just the
operators.

Hey Safin,

Small world - eXp to Tech pipeline is real! I'm on vacation this week but happy to help with specific questions once I'm back.

For SWE roles, here's the truth: Show me what you've built. Not your degree, not your certifications - your actual projects. What problems got you excited enough to lose sleep coding? That passion shows up in interviews way more than perfect leetcode.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools, YouTube tutorials, and documentation. The differentiator?Curiosity and actually shipping things. Build something weird. Break it. Fix it. Ship it.

And honestly? Coming from real estate, you already know the cheat code - it's all relationships. The best SWE role I've seen filled? Through a coffee chat, not an application. Keep having conversations with people doing what you want to do. Tech pretends it's different from real estate, but it's the same game - people hire people they know and trust.

Bryan

I have been deep on image models lately because of my corporate job. I also have a deeper understanding of generating documents like Word Excel and PowerPoint files. So my current investment is creating a listing kit tool for real estate agent agents in order to create all of the assets, and they may need for a listing in one go. The goal is to consolidate a bunch of other tools that are out there in the market into one platform. Think of combining things like AI generated video tours, AI staging and other AI marketing assets.

That sounds super exciting. You really had an awesome background that prepared you to ride the AI wave in enabling others.

I’m all about conversational agents. I was honestly working on a side project for myself for an automated phone Agent since I kept getting too many calls as a real estate agent.

Hey Rick,

Thanks for connecting! Curious how you found me, but glad you did.

I see you're running BizFlow AI - AI consulting for SMBs. How's that journey going? Tried to learn more on your site but got hit with the double modal for lead capture and couldn't find too much info.

I'm on a similar path, building AI solutions for SMBs in real estate. Debating whether to offer consulting alongside the product. Since you've been hard at work on your own firm, I would love to hear what's working and what's been challenging.

How are you finding adoption with your SMB clients?

I haven’t used Replit in a while, but it’s a solid AI-assisted cloud IDE. It’s not a drop-in replacement for n8n—tools like n8n/Zapier/Make are for wiring apps together with minimal code, whereas Replit is for building and hosting custom apps (where you can add automation yourself). Nevertheless, would love to hear your thoughts after you try it! 

If you're exploring coding tools I think I still have a promo for Lovable to try their paid version for a month if you're interested.Have any of you used Replit yet?  A friend just mentioned it to me and that it is n8n on steroids and easier user experience that doesn’t break as easily. Lmk! I am going to give it a try.
conversationTitle: (Panel Session) Democratizing AI in Real Estate: Training the Next Generation

text:
Hey Jason!

I'm excited to be on the panel with you today - can't wait to have that conversation! I saw you've built Get Social Done to give companies an entire team for less than one employee. That's exactly the kind of efficiency I'm trying to bring to real estate.

I'm always curious for another perspective- with your background, what's your take on the biggest barrier to AI adoption? Is it the tech itself or the change management?

I'm finding agents want the results but struggle with the workflow changes.

Coming from enterprise AI myself, I'd love to compare notes on what translates from big company implementations to SMBs.

Hey Malay,

I'm excited to be on the panel with you today - can't wait to have that conversation! I saw you've written extensively on AI applications in business and are helping organizations actually implement AI at scale.

I'm curious about your perspective, in real estate, we have this interesting challenge where agents are eager for AI but resistant to changing workflows. Coming from enterprise AI implementation myself, I've found the adoption curve totally different than traditional B2B SaaS. What patterns have you seen work for getting non-technical industries to actually embrace AI beyond the initial excitement? 

Would love to hear your thoughts on the call and compare notes on what's working across industries.

I’m a little confused. I’m reading through the different ticket types advertised on the website.

“””
Standard Pass - €49.99
Gain 2-day live virtual access to 18 keynote and panel sessions, connect with 0+ speakers, join live Q&As, and receive a Certificate of Participation.
​
Premium Pass - €79.99
Enjoy 2-day Premium virtual access, connect with 50+ speakers, join live Q&As, download 20+ session resources, and continue networking post-event.
​

On-Demand Pass - €99.99
Get lifetime access to 6 hours of on-demand video content, downloadable resources, post-event networking, and a Certificate of Completion.
“””

Hey Natia.

Sure thing. Please let me know if any modifications are needed.

Bio:
Bryan Plaza is an AI product lead focused on turning pilots into production. He’s shipped AI features at Microsoft and coached teams on the habits that drive ROI—clear metrics, solid data foundations, and disciplined change management. Today he applies those lessons to real estate, advising startups and operators on where AI actually moves the P&L and how to get from demo to dependable daily use.

One Line:
Bryan Plaza—an AI product lead who turns pilots into production and is bringing that playbook to real-estate startups and operators.

Hey Natia — thanks for the follow-up and for thinking of me.

I’m open to joining the panel. I can bring a practical enterprise-AI → real estate angle (ROI, RAG/data quality, change management). Since you’re finalizing the agenda, I want to make sure I’m genuinely useful for the audience and we have the basics set.

Could you share a few quick details?

Fit & format
	•	Panel title/angle, moderator, and fellow panelists
	•	Date/time (ET) and duration; live vs. recorded; Q&A or no Q&A
	•	Prep expectations

Logistics
	•	Platform and tech rehearsal (if any)
	•	Will there be a recording and captions/transcript that can be shared?

Audience & promotion
	•	Expected live + on-demand reach and attendee profile
	•	Since I wasn’t in the initial lineup post, would there be an update on the website/agenda and social mentions once confirmed?

Nice-to-haves that would help me share the session afterward
	•	Non-exclusive use of the recording (full video + 1–2 short clips) and the transcript

Thanks again!

Hey Natia. I had some time to review and I wanted to give you a response to your question.

Based on the agenda the best value that I could bring to an event like this would be in the “AI is the New Architect: Reimagining Real Estate from the Ground Up”. My background in GenAI applications B2B and B2C productivity apps combined with my real estate experience and upcoming Gen AI applications to RE. But aside from this pannel, I don’t see a good spot for me in the current openings. Thank you for reaching out.

It’s a tale as old as time. I go to check and now it’s working!

I’ll take a look. First thoughts, it’s a shame it’s only virtual. I am working on a startup and know in person events are a higher ROI for these kinds of events for me. 

Regardless, thanks for the share.

Hey Sue,

  I'm excited to be on the panel with you today - can't wait to have that conversation! I saw that
  you've dug deep into n8n workflows and other ways to leverage AI automatically and agentically. I'm
   excited to see what else you're working on.

  I'm deep in the same rabbit hole to leverage AI to make agents more efficient for their clients. Would love to compare notes on what's actually working in production sometime.

Hey Maurice,

I'm excited to be on the panel with you today - can't wait to have that conversation! I saw you're helping agents with tech stack optimization and smart workflows.

I'm curious - what are the top 2-3 pain points you're seeing that agents desperately need automated but don't realize AI can handle? I've been building solutions around listing prep and lead follow-up, but wondering what gets your clients genuinely excited.

  I come from building AI at enterprise scale (working at Microsoft) so happy to share any perspectives on what translates well from big tech to real estate SMBs if that's helpful.

Would love to compare notes sometime!

Hi Wayne! Been in big tech PM role since we last spoke Feb last year. Your MVP framework advice was spot on. As I plan my exit from corporate, working on a new real estate tech idea. Not ready yet, but would value your feedback when it is. Thanks for the early guidance - it really helped!

In the meantime, I am quietly working on my own startup venture. If you do have any opportunities that you think would would be a good match, I would love to chat more.
conversationTitle: ByteDance/TikTok: Interested in Product Manager-Dubai (UAE) position?

text:
It looks like you still got some time I would honestly say target internships as much as you can because the new grad pipeline without having an internship at Microsoft is extremely difficult. 

But in general, my best advice is focus on companies that you think are extremely cool and try to talk to as many people at that company.

Hey Sai. I’ve honestly been heads down trying to figure out what’s next for me so sorry for the delay.

I think it’s really helpful to see everything in context. For a project geared towards getting hired at Microsoft I would say focus on your technical skills, being creative, and innovative. Don’t really overthink it with real user impact or having actual user adoption. That’s a hurdle that you can get through later what the project should really highlight is your passion and adoption of new tools. Now, if there’s a specific product family that you’d like to target within Microsoft, then I’d say focus on something that relates to that product group. But regardless the skills that you’ll learn along the way by doing projects like this are completely transferable!

Hey Sai.Keep on the journey! 

It's definitely hard to get experience, especially coming from new grad. My best advice is to just work on projects yourself and get the experience that you think you need. I originally got a software engineering internship and then created a mental health organization at my school that helped me pivot into product planning in some way also worked on several projects that gave me a better product view. I think there's a unlimited number of ways that you can get to where you want to be, but that really comes down to you, period. I'd say if you're looking for any specific advice, shoot me the question and I'll try to help as much as I can.

Hey Aleksandar. I just got back from my holiday to hop on LinkedIn. It was great to meet you too! Hoping to make it back to London soon.

Just a few days ago I was hearing somewhere about a startup working to make achieving product compliance for startups go from a many month timeline to weeks and I remembered your startup. Excited to see what you get done in the space!

Thank you for the kind words Swapnaa! Now that’s what I call serendipity. 

You’re completely right the choice is still mine. It’s tough to stay motivated lately but you’ll see me keep going. 

I’m really glad to hear you’re all ok all things considered. I truly believe good things will come your way.

Hey Swapnaa. I’ve been thinking a bit about you and couldn’t let it go any longer without actually reaching out. 

I was really sad to see you go in May. These past few months has left me re-evaluating my motivations and desires lately to make sure I’m spending my energy efficiently. I sometimes look back at the conversation we had during our 1:1 and since then I’ve always been impressed by how fitting you were to your work. Back then I think I mentioned that I wanted to prioritize measurable positive human impact from my work and I’m working on holding myself to it. 

I genuinely hope you and you’re family are safe and healthy and wish you the best on whatever comes next. Thank you for inspiring me.

Hey Joyce! It was a pleasure meeting you at the event. I was honestly so happy to be there because seeing the work that is done by organizations like Lifewire is truly energizing.

I have thought about going vegetarian but unfortunately I haven’t made the jump yet. My friend Taylore on the other hand has done her tour of Seattle for the best places. Hope we run into each other soon!

Hey Aruna. Sorry for the delay here. Sadly, I had my luggage stolen while traveling and had to get new devices. I’m up and running now. I’m not sure if I will be around by then just yet, but I may be until the first week of October. I’ll give you a heads up as soon as I know.

Hello Jaouad,

I hope this message finds you well. I'm reaching out to you regarding your domain, NestQuest.com. I am currently working on a new project and the domain fits well with the future vision for this project.

Understanding your expertise lies in data science, I’m curious if you’d consider discussing the possibility of transferring this domain. I'll be honest, I am still a student while persuing other projects but I'd still like to fairly compensate you for the domain.

I respect the value and history attached to the domain, hence I am committed to honoring its legacy in its future use. If you’re open to this conversation, I’d be grateful to discuss further at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for considering my proposal. I look forward to the possibility of a positive collaboration.

Warm regards,
Bryan Plaza
(973) 932-9336
Bsplaza@outlook.com

Hi Bryan,

I hope you're having a lovely day so far!

My name is Audrey and I'm a recruiter at Epic, the healthcare software company in Madison, WI. Our software touches around 75% of the U.S. population and is used by doctors around the world. At Epic, we look for avid problem solvers with a desire to make an impact on a global scale.

I came across your profile and noticed you might be a good fit for our Technical Solutions Engineer position. You’ll work on software that impacts 250 million patients around the world. Alongside mentors and co-workers, you’ll tackle mission-critical and complex problems – we’re way beyond “turn it off and on again” issues. You’ll need to diagnose the problem, identify a solution, and manage the implementation of the solution, sometimes across multiple physical locations and stakeholders.

Please let me know if you are interested either way!

Audrey Thompson
Project Manager and Recruiting Partner at Epic
